lesson.menu_desc: Interrupt politely, raise a point and bring the meeting back on track -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 12 -- Keeping the Meeting on Track -- Interrupting Politely
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Percival JR | Aula 12 -- Keeping the Meeting on Track

listenings.text: Good morning, everyone. We are halfway through the agenda, so let me keep us on track. I know the budget is important, but that is off topic for now. We will get back to it after the safety report. If you want to raise a point, please go ahead, but keep it brief. We only have ten minutes left for this topic. So let's move on to the supplier decision and finish on time. Thank you for your patience.

Hi Percival, this is Karen. I am sorry I have to interrupt your day with a quick message. In our last meeting we went off topic and ran out of time, so I want to suggest something. Could we set a time limit for each point? If someone wants to raise a new idea, we write it down and get back to it later. I think this will help us stay on track and finish on time. Let me know what you think before tomorrow. Thanks.

extra_audio.text: First, a colleague goes off topic during the meeting. Then, Percival interrupts politely and asks to keep it brief. After that, he says they will get back to the new point later. Later, he brings the team back on track. Finally, they move on and finish the agenda on time.
